news.title: Manasum Senior Living plans to invest ₹150 crore to develop senior living project in Chennai
news.para: Manasum Senior Living is investing ₹150 crore in Chennai for The Madras Pavilion, a senior living project. This development will feature 92 villas and 36 apartments. The company also plans a ₹550 crore investment to expand its operations across South and West India.

news.title: Vadodara civic body's built homes fall apart in 20 years
news.para: Residents of Vadodara's JNNURM housing schemes are experiencing poor living conditions. Houses built for the economically weaker sections are showing signs of damage. Issues like water seepage and falling plaster have been reported. The Vadodara Municipal Corporation states the core structures are sound. They attribute problems to seepage and neglect by residents.

news.title: Karnataka HC says sub-registrars must update documents within four weeks
news.para: Karnataka High Court has issued new guidelines for civil and commercial courts and sub-registrars.  These rules aim to speed up the updating of property documents when sale deeds are declared void.  Courts must send decrees to sub-registrars within four weeks. Sub-registrars must then update records promptly. This initiative seeks to prevent unnecessary legal battles and save valuable court time.

news.title: Government land worth ₹30 crore freed from encroachment, 12 houses demolished in Indore
news.para: Indore district administration launched a significant anti-encroachment drive, reclaiming prime government land valued at approximately Rs 30 crore. Following collector Shivam Verma's orders, authorities demolished 12 illegally constructed houses spanning 30,000 square feet in Khajrana village. The administration has vowed to continue strict action against land encroachers.

news.title: Global tensions simmer, but real estate market stable in Bengaluru
news.para: Bengaluru's real estate market remains stable despite global uncertainties, with strong local factors like job growth and infrastructure development bolstering demand. While overseas investor sentiment, particularly from the Middle East, has seen a dip, end-user demand is resilient. Developers are navigating supply-side pressures on input costs, but the market's underlying strength continues to attract investment.

news.title: Lucknow development body gets 110 applications under OTS scheme
news.para: The Lucknow Development Authority (LDA) has received 110 applications for its one-time settlement scheme, aimed at resolving dues on properties. A help desk and camps are providing on-the-spot support for allottees seeking relief on defaulted installments and interest charges. The scheme, open until July 17, covers various property types.

news.title: People in 31 villages to get ownership rights of land in 'Lal Dora' area: South Delhi MP
news.para: Ownership rights for residential land in 31 Delhi villages are coming soon. Residents of these Lal Dora areas will receive property cards. This will allow them to secure bank loans easily. Drone mapping is complete. The Delhi government is finalizing regulations. This move addresses long-standing issues regarding land regularization.

news.title: SC issues notice in contempt petition concerning unauthorized constructions in Gurugram
news.para: The Supreme Court has issued a notice in a contempt petition filed by Rajdarbar Iconic Venture concerning unauthorized constructions in Gurgaon's Chakkarpur village. The petition alleges non-compliance with a December 2024 judgment that mandated obtaining completion certificates before providing civic services. Respondents, including senior Haryana officials, have been directed to submit replies by May 7, 2026.

news.title: Welspun Enterprises picks up 4.5 lakh sq ft office space in Pune’s Hinjewadi
news.para: Welspun Enterprises has secured a significant office space deal in Pune's Hinjewadi IT hub. The company leased over 4.5 lakh square feet of office space. This transaction marks one of the larger office deals in Pune this year. The lease agreement is for five years. Hinjewadi continues to be a prime location for businesses seeking expansion.

news.title: Gurugram: 1,000 illegal house extensions taken down in three days in government drive
news.para: Gurgaon authorities have demolished illegal extensions from nearly 1,000 houses in a major drive to clear public space infringements. The action, spanning across several colonies, targets structures like ramps, guard rooms, and lawns built on public land. This exercise is a court-driven initiative to reclaim roads and green belts before a state government deadline.

news.title: Kanpur: FIR against father-son duo for cheating 29 people in PMAY scam
news.para: A father-son duo in Kanpur allegedly defrauded 29 individuals of Rs 7 lakh by falsely promising housing under the Pradhan Mantri Awaas Yojana. After months of no housing and no refunds, victims reported being harassed and threatened when seeking their money back. An FIR has now been registered following court orders.

news.title: MahaRERA clears over 10,000 housing projects in FY26
news.para: Maharashtra Real Estate Regulatory Authority (MahaRera) approved 10,379 housing projects in FY 2025-26, including fresh registrations, corrections, and timeline extensions. This surge in approvals underscores the authority's regulatory oversight and a boost to the state's real estate sector, with the Mumbai Metropolitan Region leading in project approvals.

news.title: SC asks NCLAT to take decision on 14 housing projects of debt-ridden Supertech
news.para: The Supreme Court has instructed the NCLAT to expedite the process of merging the remaining 14 housing projects of Supertech. This initiative targets the timely completion of these developments, addressing the prolonged grievances of homebuyers. The NCLAT will consider input from diverse stakeholders, including local farmers and land agencies.

news.title: PNB Housing Finance's net profit up 18.34% in FY26
news.para: PNB Housing Finance's net consolidated total income stood at ₹2,171.91 crore in Q1 FY26, a growth of 6.63 per cent from ₹2,036.78 it recorded in the similar quarter last year. Its PAT was at ₹655.80 crore in Q4 FY26 versus ₹550.38 crore in Q4 FY25, reporting a growth of 19.15 per cent.

news.title: BPTP to invest ₹1,100 crore in Greater Faridabad housing project
news.para: BPTP has launched a residential project in Greater Faridabad with an estimated investment of ₹1,100 crore and a projected GDV of ₹1,800 crore. The development will add 325 premium units in the NCR market.

news.title: MMRDA secures 216 acres in Raigad for Mumbai 3.0 project through participatory model
news.para: Mumbai 3.0 project is underway with MMRDA securing 216 acres in Raigad district. This marks a significant step in a new participatory development model. Landowners are offered various compensation options. The project aims to create a new economic hub with advanced infrastructure. It is expected to generate substantial employment and attract investment, shaping a new urban future for the region.

news.title: Vedaanta Senior Living plans to invest ₹200 crore in Kerala in next three years
news.para: Vedaanta Senior Living is set to invest ₹200 crore in Kerala over the next three years, launching its Eden Gardens project in Ettumanoor with a ₹50 crore investment. This expansion includes 48 villas and 30 apartments, with prices starting at ₹76 lakh, building on their existing presence in Guruvayur and Kochi.

news.title: Office completions fall 36% quarter-on-quarter in Q1 2026 amid West Asia tensions: Report
news.para: Office space completions declined 36% quarter-on-quarter to 9.7 million sq ft in Q1 2026, while absorption rose 20% year-on-year, indicating resilient demand despite global uncertainties. Vacancy levels tightened to 9.5% as supply moderated, according to Vestian.

news.title: Ganjam administration seizes 13 tractors in drive against illegal sand mining
news.para: Ganjam district administration conducted a late-night raid, seizing 13 sand-laden tractors involved in illegal mining from the prohibited Rushikulya riverbed. This action follows the imposition of Section 163 of BNSS, prohibiting mining activities in the area. An FIR has been lodged against vehicle owners for violating prohibitory orders.

news.title: New maintenance policy for Bengaluru development body apartments begins next month
news.para: Bangalore Development Authority will soon launch a new maintenance policy for its apartment projects. This policy aims to standardize maintenance charges and clearly define developer responsibilities. It will act as an interim framework until a formal Apartment Act is notified.
